subject: FW: today
body:
From: Lawrence Krauss [mailto:lawkrauss@gmail.com] On Behalf Of Lawrence Krauss
Sent: Friday, May 30, 2008 4:11 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Fwd: today

 

	From: Lawrence Krauss <krauss@genesis1.phys.cwru.edu>
	Date: May 30, 2008 3:37:55 PM EDT
	To: Jeffrey Epstein <littlestjeff@yahoo.com>
	Cc: Lawrence Krauss <krauss@genesis1.phys.cwru.edu>
	Subject: today

	thanks Jeffrey... I enjoyed our discussion, and will think on the marriage vs religion issue.. 

	 

	Also, are you seriously interested in meeting chomsky?  If you are I can try and work on it, but it would

	probably involve us going up to boston together..

	 

	Finally, Arizona.. I am very excited that you are excited...   My thinking is that ultimately Origins will need

	about $30 Million..  a $10 Million leadership gift, maybe even tied to the University raising matching funds

	would jump start things so we could ramp up toward full operations quickly.. but even $1M up front to seed

	activities...  If I can show Arizona that there is interest right away, that will go a long way toward the university

	moving quickly to grow this..

	 

	some thoughts about things we didn't discuss

	 

	1. one purpose will be to bring together the world's best for short periods.. i.e. months, to focus on a

	key outstanding problem..  This will be easier than trying to recruit a whole bunch of people quickly.   As

	I said, I think we can recruit a few stars with unusual offers, but the constant influx of great people will

	establish a tradition that will be important...  the symposium will help introduce everyone to the reality that

	we can get anyone there..

	 

	2. The institute NEEDs to have a summer location...  it cannot function in AZ in summer.. whether that

	is up in Flagstaff or somewhere else is something that has to be determined....    I have great interest

	expressed in Zurich in having their summer location in the mountains as a possible home... or, if I could

	convince Oxford to host a summer home that is another possibility...

	 

	Anyway, am at the airport, but wanted to get this to you... I look forward to hearing from you...  and keep

	me up to date on you as well..

	 

	Best

	 

	 

	Lawrence

	 

	 

	__________________________________

	 

	Lawrence M. Krauss

	Ambrose Swasey Prof. of Physics and Astronomy

	Director Center for Education and Research in Cosmology and Astrophysics

	Dept of Physics, CWRU

	10900 Euclid Ave, Cleveland OH 44106-7079

	krauss@genesis1.phys.cwru.edu <mailto:krauss@case.edu>    

	216 368 4070
body_html:
From: Lawrence Krauss [mailto:lawkrauss@gmail.com] On Behalf Of Lawrence Krauss
Sent: Friday, May 30, 2008 4:11 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Fwd: today

 

From: Lawrence Krauss <krauss@genesis1.phys.cwru.edu>
Date: May 30, 2008 3:37:55 PM EDT
To: Jeffrey Epstein <littlestjeff@yahoo.com>
Cc: Lawrence Krauss <krauss@genesis1.phys.cwru.edu>
Subject: today

thanks Jeffrey... I enjoyed our discussion, and will think on the marriage vs religion issue.. 

 

Also, are you seriously interested in meeting chomsky?  If you are I can try and work on it, but it would

probably involve us going up to boston together..

 

Finally, Arizona.. I am very excited that you are excited...   My thinking is that ultimately Origins will need

about $30 Million..  a $10 Million leadership gift, maybe even tied to the University raising matching funds

would jump start things so we could ramp up toward full operations quickly.. but even $1M up front to seed

activities...  If I can show Arizona that there is interest right away, that will go a long way toward the university

moving quickly to grow this..

 

some thoughts about things we didn't discuss

 

1. one purpose will be to bring together the world's best for short periods.. i.e. months, to focus on a

key outstanding problem..  This will be easier than trying to recruit a whole bunch of people quickly.   As

I said, I think we can recruit a few stars with unusual offers, but the constant influx of great people will

establish a tradition that will be important...  the symposium will help introduce everyone to the reality that

we can get anyone there..

 

2. The institute NEEDs to have a summer location...  it cannot function in AZ in summer.. whether that

is up in Flagstaff or somewhere else is something that has to be determined....    I have great interest

expressed in Zurich in having their summer location in the mountains as a possible home... or, if I could

convince Oxford to host a summer home that is another possibility...

 

Anyway, am at the airport, but wanted to get this to you... I look forward to hearing from you...  and keep

me up to date on you as well..

 

Best

 

 

Lawrence

 

 

__________________________________

 

Lawrence M. Krauss

Ambrose Swasey Prof. of Physics and Astronomy

Director Center for Education and Research in Cosmology and Astrophysics

Dept of Physics, CWRU
